name: Streaming · Football scoreboard (Flask + Preact)
description: Real-time football scoreboard for broadcast: Flask + Socket.IO backend, Preact + TypeScript frontend, and room-scoped realtime—clear event contracts and LAN-oriented hardening guidance.
longDescription: A dual-runtime scoreboard system: Python Flask with Socket.IO powers rooms, file watchers, and asset management, while a Preact SPA renders responsive overlay scenes for streaming software. Features include multi-field support, timers, sponsor rotation, penalty boards, intro sequences, and modular components for customization. The split backend/frontend architecture keeps transport concerns (rooms, reconnects, payload shapes) documented so OBS reloads and control-desk restarts recover predictably. PyInstaller tooling lives alongside the backend for packaging when operators need executables.
features: Documented Socket.IO event payloads for control desk ↔ overlay contract stability
File watchers to bridge control-desk tools and web state
Preact components for scoreboard, sponsors, intros, and penalties
highlights: Clear split between realtime server and lightweight Preact UI—operational resilience under reloads
Designed for multi-match venues with parallel fields
Packaging path for teams that prefer standalone binaries
Security posture assumes trusted broadcast LANs unless control endpoints are authenticated
challenges: Hybrid Python + JS stacks must agree on schema evolution and reconnect behaviour when OBS reloads browser sources.
solutions: Socket.IO rooms, documented event payloads, and resilient client bootstrapping on the Preact side.
security: Run Flask behind TLS in production LANs
Authenticate control endpoints if exposed outside trusted networks
